id.description: Stable kebab-case identifier; must match the directory name.

kind.description: theorem: a whole self-contained theorem (default if absent). leaf: one lemma in a decomposition. assembly: the glue proving the main theorem from leaf lemmas.

difficulty.description: Estimated effort to one-shot (1 = trivial arithmetic/inversion … 5 = hard core). Lets contributors pick what they can finish.

depends_on.description: Ids of leaves whose STATEMENTS this unit may assume (not their proofs). Used to order assembly and to build a leaf's self-contained prompt.

claim_kind.description: correctness: the algorithm computes the right answer. complexity/closed-form: an asymptotic or exact cost bound. worst-case/best-case: a tight upper/lower bound over all inputs of size n. expected-cost: the exact mean cost under a stated input distribution (the verify-track twin of a conjectured E[cost(n)]). distribution/limit-law: the exact (or limiting) law of the cost — these are computed on the CONJECTURE track and only the provable consequences (e.g. the mean) are promoted to theorems.

analysis.description: Which case is analysed and, for average/distribution/limit claims, the input distribution it is taken over. Required for best/worst/expected/distribution/limit kinds.

case.description: worst/best: extremal over inputs of size n. average: mean over the input distribution. distribution: the full law of the cost. limit: the limiting law of the standardized cost as n grows.

input_distribution.description: The distribution inputs are drawn from, e.g. "uniform present target in {1..n}" or "uniformly random permutation of n elements". Omit for worst/best (extremal cases need no distribution).

conjecture_artifact.description: Path (repo-relative) to the conjecture-track result this unit is the verified twin of, e.g. tools/conjecture/results/linear-search.json. Orthogonal to claim_kind: a closed-form sum, an expected-cost, or a distribution's mean can each link back to its computed evidence.

expected_theorem.description: Name of the identifier a submission must prove with no axioms (checked by the verifier via Print Assumptions). For now a single canonical name shared across backends.

cost_model.description: How a step is costed. Required for complexity/closed-form claims.

kind.description: func-ops: operation count on a functional core; while-ops: operation count on a WHILE imperative core; riscv/llvm: concrete-cost layers.

counts.description: Designated operations counted as unit cost, e.g. ["comparison"], ["key-comparison"], ["multiplication"].
